ground_truth.title: Squad 2024 - 2025
ground_truth.author: Spursidol
ground_truth.main_content:
Spursidol
30 Jun 2024

Opening post of this thread is correct at 31 August (end of PL transfer window) and 13 September (date when PL squads are registered, and after date of registration of EL squad)

Squad after:
Players In - Gray, Bergvall, Solanke and Odobert signings, Werner loan signing.
Players Out - Rodon, Hojbjerg, Royal, Skipp & Lo Celso exits, Gil, Veliz & Solomon on loan
and Spence seemingly rehabilitated

Under UEFA definitions Spurs have a maximum allowed squad of is 21 players plus any Club Trained players and youngsters qualifying to be on List B

With our current squad we needed to omit 2 players, Reguilon, and Spence to get to that UEFA compliant squad size (squad shown below).

In PL and domestic cups we will probably see the squad complying with UEFA rules plus any number of u21's including Gray, Bergvall and Odobert who do not require registration and thus Spence can be included in the PL squad. (And the PL squad also has space to include Reguilon and Robson due to the designation of Gray, Bergvall and Odobert as u21 and therefore outside the requirement of registration in the 25 man squad)

In order to go deep in PL EL and domestic cups playing 60+ games, we would struggle so will keep u21's who can be registered as List B players in the squad - Lankshear (striker), and Moore (RW/LW) will add to squad depth without requiring registration - and if each plays say 1,000+ minutes each (11 full match equivalent) that's decent development for the players

GK - Vicario/Forster/Austin/Whiteman or Gunter
RB - Porro (and Spence (also LB) in PL squad)
CB - Romero/Van De Venn/Dragusin/Gray (also DM & RB)
LB - Udogie/Davies (also LCB)
CM1 - Bissouma
CM2 - Bentancur/Sarr
CM3 - Maddison/Bergvall
RW - Kulusevski/Johnson/Moore
LW - Son/Werner(loan), Odobert
CF - Solanke/Richarlison/Lankshear

Analysis under UEFA definitions:

Club Trained (1 or 2)
Austin (3rd GK) (Minimum 4 for full 25 man squad) plus Whiteman (Or Gunter as List B player)

Trained at Other Clubs affiliated to The FA and FAW (6)
Maddison, Johnson, Forster, Davies, Gray, Solanke (and Spence in PL squads)

Overseas Trained (15)
Vicario, Romero, Van de Ven, Dragusin, Porro, Bissouma, Bentancur, Bergvall, Richarlison, Son, Kulusevski, Werner (loan), Udogie, Sarr, Odobert

Under 21's/List B (3 listed but can be any number)
Lankshear, Moore (possibly including Gunter)

Spursidol
30 Jun 2024

So the likely leavers:

Hojbjerg, Lo Celso, Gil, Solomon (all Overseas trained players in last season's squad) and sell players loaned out last season - Rodon, Spence, Reguilon

and that's before we look at upgrading a few players in some positions.

So that could mean trying to shift about a dozen players to get maybe 6 players in - a lot of work to be done before window closes!

sudoku
1 Jul 2024

JULY 2:
Edit: Rodon gone and Gray official.
JULY 3:
Edit: Parrott officially in First Team Squad. Robson remains in the U21s.
Devine and Scarlett added to 1st team squad.

Here is a full list of all 35 players currently included in the 1st team squad

Auscock
1 Jul 2024

Pretty grim when you consider that 3 or 4 of the CT/HG are expected to be sold.

sudoku
1 Jul 2024

With HG the big question is will Ange keep Skipp and promote Parrott?

Let's assume they both are included as CT along with Whiteman and Austin to give us our full CT quota for UEFA games.

Robson will likely be kept as an over age player in the U21s or potentially as the 4th CT man if Whiteman leaves.
Edit: Parrott and Skipp transferred. Robson would be a "free pick" for EL games.

sudoku
1 Jul 2024

Of our association players and transferred U21s you would think that Spence will not be included in the squad and is highly likely to sold. Davies could well leave as well.
Loans likely for Phillips and Vuskovic and probably Veliz although I think not a full season loan for him.

Edit: July 22: Vuskovic on loan.

sudoku
1 Jul 2024

NHG:

Royal, Reguillon and Gil will definitely leave and GLC and Hojbjerg are highly likely to also go. Potentially Solomon or even Bissouma?

If 5 left that would leave us with 14 NHG for EPL and 17 for UEFA so in theory we could register all our NHG U21s Bergvall, Vuskovic and Veliz. Of course Vuskovic highly likely to be loaned.

In the EPL none of our U21s need be included in the squad of 25.

Edit: July 22. Vuskovic loaned and Hojbjerg transferred.

Auscock
1 Jul 2024

Makes sense to bring back Edwards or KWP as this would help both CT & HG

Spursidol
1 Jul 2024

Auscock said:
Makes sense to bring back Edwards or KWP as this would help both CT & HG

Spurs have a lot of very good forward players in u21's, so not sure it helps to bring back Edwards.

KWP makes more sense as we are light in full back positions, but with Archie Gray very likely joining (who can cover RB), part of the rationale goes away, although for EL Gray will count as an 'association trained' player for first 2 seasons, List B/u21 in 3rd season and club trained after that. But with Southampton back in PL the player may need to be persuaded?

Spursidol
1 Jul 2024

I think we take it as full confirmation that Gray has joined and Rodon left!

Park Lane Mark Twain
2 Jul 2024

Players 24 or under:

Porro
Dragusin
Van De Ven
Udogie
Sarr
Gray
Bergvall
Johnson
Kulusevski

Many more in and around the set up as well like Vuskovic, Phillips, Hall, Moore, Veliz etc.

We have plenty of talent now with high ceilings of growth. Neto, Calafiori and Eze next Daniel ta very much.

bonaspur23
2 Jul 2024

That group of forwards is looking rough and the only 2 midfielders I trust are Sarr and Maddison. Maybe Bentancur if he recovers physically.

A lot of work to do.

Starks
2 Jul 2024

Park Lane Mark Twain said:
Players 24 or under:
Porro, Dragusin, Van De Ven, Udogie, Sarr, Gray, Bergvall, Johnson, Kulusevski
We have plenty of talent now with high ceilings of growth. Neto, Calafiori and Eze next Daniel ta very much.

Unbelievable recruitment over the last few years tbh.
